prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "靠墙静蹲" (wall sit) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person in a wall-sit isometric hold: back, shoulders, and head pressed flat against a vertical wall; feet shoulder-width apart and flat on the ground, positioned about a thigh-length out from the wall; knees bent at exactly 90 degrees with thighs parallel to the floor; shins vertical; arms hanging relaxed at sides or crossed at chest, plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: quadriceps (front of thighs) holding the isometric position. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "股四头肌" pointing to the quadriceps holding the position; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus; "核心肌群" pointing to the core muscles. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
